channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Woman saves £30 on luggage fees by mailing her clothes ahead of a flight
item.description: Laura Poole, a 33-year-old confidence trainer from Cardiff, avoided paying a £30 luggage fee by posting her clothes for just £2.59. She shipped several outfits, including dresses and suit jackets, usi...

item.title: U.S.-Israel Conflict: Analyzing the Impact on Iran's Leadership
item.description: The ongoing U.S.-Israel attacks on Iran have escalated over the past few days, causing significant damage and fatalities within Iran's leadership structure. Hundreds have been killed, and retaliatory ...

item.title: ChatGPT Sees Massive Spike in Uninstalls Following Department of Defense Deal
item.description: The mobile app uninstalls of ChatGPT saw a staggering 295% jump on February 28, 2026, in the U.S., following news of OpenAI's partnership with the U.S. Department of Defense (DoD). This spike marked a...

item.title: Dirty Business highlights the corruption and failures within England's water industry
item.description: A new Channel 4 docudrama, 'Dirty Business', delves into the serious issues surrounding the privatised English water industry, focusing on the investigation of sewage discharges into local rivers by t...

item.title: James Webb Telescope Discovers Distant Jellyfish Galaxy with Star Formation
item.description: Astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) have identified the most distant jellyfish galaxy ever observed. This galaxy, located 8.5 billion light-years away, exhibits long, flowing trail...

item.title: Supreme Court Imposes Penalty on Employer for Delaying Compensation Payment
item.description: In a significant ruling, the Supreme Court has emphasized that employers must bear the penalty for delays in depositing compensation under the Employees' Compensation Act, 1923. The judgment was deliv...

item.title: Tech Entrepreneur Ethan Agarwal Challenges Ro Khanna for California's 17th Congressional District
item.description: Ethan Agarwal, a 40-year-old tech entrepreneur, has announced his candidacy for California’s 17th Congressional District, challenging incumbent Representative Ro Khanna. Agarwal, who has no political ...

item.title: Joseph Kony and LRA: Survival in African Borderlands for Decades
item.description: Joseph Kony, leader of the Lord’s Resistance Army (LRA), still remains free more than twenty years after the International Criminal Court (ICC) issued arrest warrants for him and some of his commander...

item.title: Advanced Plasma Sensors Could Be Key to Unlocking Commercial Fusion Power
item.description: Fusion energy has the potential to be a significant source of clean energy, but researchers face challenges in measuring the extreme and fast-moving plasmas necessary for fusion reactions. A new repor...

item.title: Shortage of Public Defenders Results in Hundreds of Criminal Case Dismissals in Oregon and Across the U.S.
item.description: Oregon's Supreme Court recently ruled that more than 1,400 criminal cases must be dismissed due to a severe shortage of public defenders. This issue isn't isolated to Oregon; across the U.S., many sta...

item.title: Australia Doubles Graduate Visa Fees and Introduces Other Changes
item.description: Australia has announced a significant increase in application fees for the 485 graduate visa, a visa that allows international students to stay and work in Australia for two years post-graduation. Fro...

item.title: Building Sustainable Habits: How Connecting with Your Values Makes Change Last
item.description: In this article, Dr. Jennifer Caspari explains why relying on willpower alone is often insufficient for building lasting habits. Instead of focusing on self-control, Caspari advocates for connecting n...

item.title: PDEA Declares Cotabato City’s LGU Operations Center Drug-Free
item.description: On March 2, 2026, the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) officially declared the People’s Palace in Cotabato City as a drug-free workplace. The declaration was made during a formal ceremony hel...

item.title: House Committee Approves Anti-Political Dynasty Bill, Sees It as Major Step in Political Reform
item.description: The House Committee on Suffrage and Electoral Reforms, led by Rep. Zia Alonto Adiong, has approved a substitute bill aimed at addressing the issue of political dynasties in the Philippines. Adiong des...

item.title: Prominent Nigerians Not Giving Money Away for Ramadan - Scam Exposed
item.description: A number of social media posts have been circulating claiming that prominent Nigerians, including billionaire Aliko Dangote and politicians Isa Ali Ibrahim Pantami and Saliu Mustapha, are giving away ...

item.title: How the Iran Conflict is Affecting UK Energy Prices and Consumer Bills
item.description: The ongoing Iran conflict is having significant economic ramifications that are already pushing up global oil and gas prices. The Strait of Hormuz, a key oil trade route, has been affected by Iranian ...

item.title: Iranian Canadians in Regina Consider Impacts of Escalating Middle East Tensions
item.description: As tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate, Iranian Canadians living in Regina are closely watching events unfold and reflecting on the implications for themselves and their families. The rec...

item.title: Niger and France's Growing Tensions: What’s Behind the Conflict?
item.description: Tensions between Niger and France have significantly increased following the attack on Diori Hamani International Airport and Air Base in Niamey. Niger's government has publicly accused France of bein...

item.title: Journalist's Interview with Ian Huntley Sparks Police Suspicion After Girls' Disappearance
item.description: In the aftermath of the 2002 disappearance of schoolgirls Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in Soham, BBC journalist Brian Farmer recounted his interview with Ian Huntley, the caretaker who would later ...

item.title: Reporting From Iran Amid Internet Blackouts: How Journalists Are Adapting
item.description: After Israeli and American strikes killed senior Iranian officials in Tehran, the Iranian government imposed a near-total internet blackout. This move has severely impacted journalists and activists i...
